---
title: "Week-12-Activity-Patrick-Torralba"
author: "Patrick Torralba"
format: html
editor: visual
---


```{r}
library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(bslib)
library(bsicons)

# UI Logic
ui <- page_sidebar(
  title = "Power of a One-Sample T-Test",
  theme = bs_theme(version = 5, bootswatch = "flatly"),
  
  sidebar = sidebar(
    title = "Parameters",
    sliderInput("n", "Sample Size (n):", min = 2, max = 100, value = 30),
    
    # Tooltip for True Mean
    sliderInput("mu_alt", "True Mean (Alternative μ):", min = 0, max = 5, value = 2, step = 0.1),
    tooltip(
      bs_icon("question-circle-fill", size = "1.2em"),
      "The True Mean defines the shift from the Null (μ=0). A larger shift increases Power.",
      placement = "right"
    ),
    
    sliderInput("sd", "Standard Deviation (σ):", min = 0.5, max = 5, value = 1, step = 0.1),
    sliderInput("alpha", "Significance Level (α):", min = 0.01, max = 0.2, value = 0.05, step = 0.01)
  ),

  # Main Panel
  layout_columns(
    fill = FALSE,
    value_box(
      title = "Statistical Power (1 - β)",
      value = textOutput("power_val"),
      showcase = bs_icon("lightning-fill"),
      theme = "primary"
    )
  ),
  
  card(
    full_screen = TRUE,
    card_header("Distribution Visualization"),
    plotOutput("power_plot")
  )
)
```


```{r}
# Server Logic
server <- function(input, output) {
  
  # Calculate Statistical Power
  power_calc <- reactive({
    # Standard Error
    se <- input$sd / sqrt(input$n)
    # Critical Value (one-tailed for simplicity in visualization)
    z_crit <- qnorm(1 - input$alpha, mean = 0, sd = se)
    # Power is the area under the alternative curve to the right of z_crit
    pwr <- 1 - pnorm(z_crit, mean = input$mu_alt, sd = se)
    return(list(pwr = pwr, z_crit = z_crit, se = se))
  })

  output$power_val <- renderText({
    paste0(round(power_calc()$pwr * 100, 1), "%")
  })

  output$power_plot <- renderPlot({
    res <- power_calc()
    se <- res$se
    z_crit <- res$z_crit
    mu_a <- input$mu_alt
    
    # Define range for plot to keep both distributions visible
    x_min <- min(-3 * se, mu_a - 3 * se)
    x_max <- max(3 * se, mu_a + 4 * se)
    x <- seq(x_min, x_max, length.out = 500)
    
    # Dataframes for plotting
    df_null <- data.frame(x = x, y = dnorm(x, mean = 0, sd = se))
    df_alt <- data.frame(x = x, y = dnorm(x, mean = mu_a, sd = se))
```


```{r}
ggplot() +
      # Null Distribution
      geom_line(data = df_null, aes(x, y, color = "Null (μ=0)"), linewidth = 1) +
      # Alternative Distribution
      geom_line(data = df_alt, aes(x, y, color = "Alt (True μ)"), linewidth = 1) +
      
      # Shade Alpha (Critical Region)
      geom_area(data = subset(df_null, x >= z_crit), aes(x, y, fill = "Alpha (Type I Error)"), alpha = 0.4) +
      
      # Shade Power
      geom_area(data = subset(df_alt, x >= z_crit), aes(x, y, fill = "Power (1 - Beta)"), alpha = 0.4) +
      
      # Critical Value Line
      geom_vline(xintercept = z_crit, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
      annotate("text", x = z_crit, y = max(df_null$y), label = "Critical Value", angle = 90, vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
      
      labs(title = "Null vs. Alternative Hypothesis Distributions",
           subtitle = paste("Effect Size shift =", mu_a),
           x = "Sample Mean", y = "Density", fill = "Regions", color = "Distributions") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Alpha (Type I Error)" = "#e74c3c", "Power (1 - Beta)" = "#2ecc71")) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("Null (μ=0)" = "#7f8c8d", "Alt (True μ)" = "#2980b9")) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom",
            plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```
